methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Arc Boats is a Series B-stage transportation company founded by Mitch Lee and Ryan Cook that has raised over $100M to date. The comparable universe consists of seven private frontier-transport firms including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, The ePlane Company, and DeepDrive; no public transportation peers provide sufficient comparability at this stage and valuation tier.

Due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage peers within the transportation sector—fewer than three datapoints meet narrow filtering criteria—peer-median valuation was not applicable. The primary method deployed is stage-progression analysis, which applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $100M total-raised proxy, reflecting typical step-up dynamics from Series B forward. This yields an implied valuation of $190M.

The triangulated range is $162M (low) to $218M (high), with a median of $190M. The analysis rests on a single methodological pillar, creating meaningful uncertainty; stronger conviction would require either additional same-stage comparable data or validation through revenue multiples or discounted-cash-flow sensitivity. Key risks to valuation include definition of the addressable market within transportation, capital intensity of commercialization, and competitive positioning relative to the named peer set.
